notes: Evidence snippets directly support the claim. Source is official Cambridge Information Compliance Office guidance. Claim is a faithful summary of data input restrictions. Boundary note: this describes data protection guidance, not legal advice on GDPR compliance.

Evidence supports claim. Source states 'Copilot, Gemini and NotebookLM'; claim adds 'Microsoft 365' and 'Google' prefixes which are accurate product names. Procurement requirement and free-version prohibition both supported by evidence. Boundary note: describes procurement process, not legal advice.

Evidence snippet directly supports both parts: licensed tools not used for training, and free tools risk training use. Source is official Cambridge guidance. Boundary note: describes data handling policy, not legal advice on data protection law.
Evidence snippets (4a and 4b) collectively support all three claim elements: human evaluation of outputs, acknowledgment requirement, and staff responsibility. Source is official Cambridge guidance.
Approved after Firecrawl captured direct Cambridge Information Compliance text covering purpose limitation and human involvement for automated decision-making.
Evidence snippets (6a and 6b) support both elements: DPIA/ISRA as relevant risk assessment processes, and AUP continuing to apply with compliance monitoring. Source is official Cambridge guidance.

Evidence snippet is an exact match with claim text. Source is official Cambridge Blended Learning Service. Boundary note: this describes academic misconduct policy. The tracker does not provide academic integrity advice; institutions define their own misconduct procedures.

Evidence snippet matches claim text closely. Source is official Cambridge guidance. Boundary note: describes Cambridge's position on AI detection tools, not a universal recommendation. The tracker does not provide academic integrity advice.
Approved after Firecrawl captured direct Cambridge assessment-page text covering both examiner prohibitions and the documented-feedback writing exception.

Evidence snippets (1a and 1b) support all claim elements: broad permission for GenAI use, student permission for study/research/formative work, and staff permission. Source is official Cambridge Blended Learning Service.

Evidence snippet supports all four principles: privacy/data awareness, ethical implications, acknowledgment requirement, and staff responsibility. Source is official Cambridge guidance. Boundary note: describes general principles, not legal or academic integrity advice.

Evidence snippet supports the framework approach (not a single centralized policy) and local determination by triposes/departments/faculties/college. The attribution to Dr Claire Benn and Dr John Burden from the Leverhulme Centre is not in the evidence snippet but is verifiable from the source page metadata. Source is official Cambridge Blended Learning Service.
